question: How does Xinpinmu change Temu's competitive positioning internationally?

findings.claim: Temu's global ad budget was ~$4.3B in 2024, running ~$1B/month, then slashed dramatically in 2025
findings.data: 2024 global ad budget ~$4.3B (36Kr report). ~$1B/month run rate. April 2025 total ad spend collapsed to $66M (Europe 49%, LatAm 16%). US ad spend cut 31% daily across FB/IG/TikTok/Snap/YouTube. Google Shopping spend went to zero by April 12. Only 6 Meta ads running in US by mid-April. US paid traffic dropped 77%. Etsy CEO said Temu/Shein were 'almost single-handedly' driving up ad costs for everyone.

findings.claim: Temu reportedly loses ~$30 per order on average, reflecting massive subsidies for user acquisition
findings.data: WIRED reported Temu loses an average of $30 per order. Industry observers flagged profitability concerns given subsidy scale. PDD stock fell ~30% from November 2025 peak. Revenue growth slowed from 59% in 2024 to single digits.

findings.claim: Temu shifting from paid to organic growth: organic search traffic rose 4.3% MoM while paid fell 6% in early 2025
findings.data: Between January and February 2025, Temu's organic search traffic rose 4.3% month-over-month, while paid search traffic fell 6.04%. This predated the tariff-driven ad cuts and suggests a strategic pivot toward organic/brand-driven growth — which Xinpinmu's owned brands would further support.

findings.claim: Temu US install base recovered from 68M (June 2025) to 91M (October 2025) despite minimal ad spend
findings.data: US install base dropped to 68M users in June 2025 after tariff shock and ad pullback. Recovered to 91M by October 2025. App open rate fell from 82% to 64%. Temu and Shein still ranked #1 and #2 globally for retail app downloads in 2025, despite US decline.
